{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Positional Information {#sec-positional-information}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- Attention is permutation-equivariant, so position must be injected. How,
     and what makes a positional scheme extrapolate. -->
:::

**Learning objectives.**

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Why order is invisible to attention

<!-- Recall permutation-equivariance from @sec-attention-as-gnn; a permuted
     input gives a permuted output. Position is a node feature we must add. -->

## Absolute positions

<!-- Learned and sinusoidal absolute encodings; limitations (fixed context,
     poor extrapolation). -->

## Relative and rotary positions

<!-- RoPE: rotating q/k by position-dependent angles so dot products depend
     on relative offset. ALiBi as an alternative bias. -->

## Length extrapolation

<!-- What breaks beyond the training context and why; a pointer to how this
     motivates some Part V architectures. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}
